thresholdOverrides.reason: intentional-policy: presentational components are covered by visual/preview review, not unit tests. CRAP's coverage weighting flags every untested component — a coverage expectation the repo has declined for this surface. Cyclomatic + cognitive stay universal, so a genuinely tangled component still fails.

intentional-policy: deploy/CLI entry scripts (the `entry` set in this config). They run at build/deploy, gated by assert:routes, the migrations, and the SDE-pipeline tests — not unit coverage. CRAP off; cognitive raised to 20 to admit the mandatory advisory-lock try/finally nesting in ingest-sde-if-empty.ts (cog 16). Cyclomatic stays at 20.

framework-convention: Next.js route handlers + middleware are a sequence of boundary guard clauses (parse -> Zod -> auth -> rate-limit -> dispatch); validation-at-the-boundary is an architecture invariant, and per policy they are unit-tested only for non-trivial logic. CRAP off; cognitive raised to 18 to admit the guard depth in contact/route.ts and dev/esi/route.ts (cog 17). Handlers with real logic get tests, not this.

framework-convention: client-side React effect / live-context modules whose nesting is mandatory effect-cleanup correctness (AbortController, per-batch try/finally, unmount drain). Integration/visual-tested; the unit-tested halves are the server engines.

intentional-policy: DB-bound data accessors. They build/run SQL against already-typed inputs (the 'queries accept typed values, validation in routes' invariant) and are verified via the consuming routes/pages and integration, not unit coverage. Cyclomatic + cognitive stay universal, so a genuinely complex query still surfaces.
framework-convention: the Better Auth provider/config object, exercised by the live auth flow rather than unit tests.
framework-convention: a client-side telemetry sender (sendBeacon/fetch over browser APIs) — fire-and-forget glue verified in the browser, not unit-tested.
intentional-policy: preview/mock fixture generators consumed only by the /preview sandbox (src/app/preview/cards), never production — fixtures aren't unit-tested.

zones.// note: First-match-wins: auth-surface is listed BEFORE the features autoDiscover zone so these 3 files classify here, not into features/auth. Verified via `fallow list --boundaries` (auth-surface=3 files, features/auth=30). Mirrors the ESLint shared-auth-surface element.
zones.name: auth-surface
